title: Forward Deployed Software Engineer (AI Agents)
company_focus: AI agents for logistics and supply chain automation
description:
About Pallet

Pallet is building AI Agents to transform the $12 trillion
logistics industry, backed by $50M from investors including
General Catalyst, Bessemer Venture Partners, and Bain
Capital Ventures. Our mission is to automate the physical
economy by creating the most reliable intelligence
infrastructure for logistics.

Our agents excel at handling complex logistics
workflows—scheduling, data entry, and load management—for
Fortune 500 companies. But this is only the beginning. Our
vision is to be the go-to answer for AI in logistics: from
proprietary models to a suite of tools that turn messy
business rules into production-ready context for LLMs.

We're positioned to build the next $10B company in
logistics. Join us and work alongside leaders from Google,
DoorDash, YC, and more to shape the future of logistics
tech.

About the Opportunity

Most engineering roles ask you to build in a controlled
environment. This one puts you in the field. We're hiring
one of our founding team of Agent Delivery Software
Engineers to embed directly with customers and deploy
production AI agents inside some of the world's largest
logistics companies. You will sit alongside customer
engineers and operations teams, reverse engineer legacy
systems, design pragmatic integrations, and ship AI
workflows into messy, real-world environments.

This is not a research or pure backend role. This is a
hands-on deployment role focused on getting systems live,
keeping them reliable, and owning real-world outcomes.

Why this role is different

- One of the founding Agent Delivery Engineers at Pallet, with
  ownership over real customer outcomes from day one
- Hands-on deployment work across some of the largest
  logistics companies in the world
- Direct collaboration with founders and a tight engineering
  team, no layers, no red tape
- Meaningful equity in a company targeting a $10B+ outcome
- Compensation and equity at the 80th percentile, plus bonuses
  tied to customer go-lives

How You Will Make an Impact

- Reverse engineer undocumented APIs, ERPs, TMS systems, and
  internal tools with minimal guidance
- Debug broken data pipelines, mismatched schemas, and flaky
  integrations in production environments
- Sit onsite with customer teams to diagnose failures and ship
  fixes in real time (roughly 25% travel)
- Make pragmatic tradeoffs to get systems live under tight
  timelines
- Own customer go-lives end-to-end, from scoping to production
  stability
- Build with observability in mind so issues surface quickly
  and stay fixed

Preferred Experience

- 2 to 4+ years of experience shipping and owning production
  systems, ideally in a high-growth or early-stage
  environment
- Strong experience integrating with third-party APIs,
  authentication systems, and event-driven workflows
- Comfortable debugging distributed systems in production
  under real customer constraints
- Thrives in ambiguous environments where requirements evolve
  and documentation is sparse
- Some experience working directly with customers or external
  stakeholders is a plus
- Genuinely enjoys messy real-world systems more than clean
  internal abstractions
- Excited to work in person 5 days a week and comfortable with
  roughly 25% travel to customer sites
- Not the right fit for those primarily interested in ML
  research, deeply scoped platform work, or remote-first
  environments

How we work

- Impact from day one: your first week might involve wiring a
  new integration into production or debugging a workflow
  that saves a customer hours
- One standup at 10 am PST. The rest is building
- Small PRs, fast iteration, and a team that sits side by side
  so questions get answered in minutes
- Catered lunch daily, Thursday boba runs, and AI tools
  actively encouraged (Claude, Cursor) as long as you
  understand the architecture underneath
- We promote from within: several teammates earned promotions
  in under a year

Our Tech Stack

- Backend: Node.js + TypeScript on Encore.dev, event-driven
  design with message passing and queues, hosted on GCP
- Database: PostgreSQL
- LLMs: OpenAI, Anthropic, Google (foundation models), fine-
  tuned models on Vertex AI
- Browser Automation: Playwright
- Frontend: Next.js + React + TypeScript, hosted on Vercel
- Observability: Datadog

Interview Process

- Intro Chat with Recruiting - (30 mins)
- Technical Screen + Meet and Greet with an Engineer (60 mins)
- Executive Chat with Bizops Lead or CTO (30-45 mins)
- Final Interview: deep-dive technical and behavioral
  interviews (~4 hours)

Location

- San Francisco: Since we're a small team building 0-1
  products, we prefer candidates to be comfortable working
  in an in-office environment in San Francisco. We typically
  come into the office 5 days a week. Relocation assistance
  can be provided for interested candidates.
- New York: Satellite Office with ~8 Pallet employees across
  engineering, operations, and success - we are seeking to
  hire and grow out the team more here. The team typically
  comes into the office 4-5 days a week. Relocation
  assistance is not provided for our New York office.
- Travel: ~25% travel to customer locations

Compensation

The estimated salary range for this role is
$130,000–$200,000, depending on experience and skill set. In
addition to base salary, we offer competitive equity,
benefits, and growth opportunities. Final compensation will
be determined based on a combination of factors, including
experience, qualifications, and location.

Top-Tier Benefits

🩺 Health, Vision, and Dental benefits

🏝️ Flexible PTO

➕ Life Insurance and Accidental Insurance

❤️‍🩹 Short-Term Disability Coverage

💸 Generous salary and equity for all staff

🪜 401k option; helping you save for the future

📚 Yearly learning and development stipend

🚌 Commuter benefits

🚘 Uber ride stipend if you ever have to work late in the
office

🏡 Office home stipend to get you comfy in your space

🍔 Daily catered lunches provided by Sharebite (every meal
you order, one meal gets donated)

🥤 Monthly happy hours

🎉 Annual Company Offsites; this year we'll be headed to Palm
Springs 🌴

Pallet is proud to be an Equal Employment Opportunity and
Affirmative Action employer. We celebrate diversity and are
committed to creating an inclusive environment for all
employees.
responsibilities: Deploy production AI agents into Fortune 500 logistics companies
Reverse engineer undocumented APIs, ERPs, TMS systems, and internal tools
Debug broken data pipelines, mismatched schemas, and flaky integrations in production
Sit onsite with customer teams to diagnose failures and ship fixes in real time
Own customer go-lives end-to-end from scoping to production stability
Build systems with observability mind for quick issue detection and resolution
use_cases: Automating logistics workflows including scheduling, data entry, and load management
AI agents for Fortune 500 logistics companies
Transforming messy business rules into production-ready context for LLMs
Intelligence infrastructure for the physical economy and logistics industry
ai_classification_reasoning:
This is a Forward Deployed Engineer role specifically
focused on deploying production AI agents into customer
environments. The role involves hands-on deployment of
AI/LLM-powered systems (agents, workflows) working directly
with foundation models (OpenAI, Anthropic) and fine-tuned
models. As noted in the instructions, FDEs who deploy AI
solutions are classified as ai-first.
